titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gerald Fogarty Galloway Collection of Records Concerning Mines and Real Property in Park County, Colorado
Records Concerning Mines and Real Property in Park County, Colorado
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 20, 2019 at 04:00

did.unittitle: Gerald Fogarty Galloway Collection of Records Concerning Mines and Real Property in Park County, Colorado

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
userestrict.p: The Gerald Fogarty Galloway Collection of Records Concerning Mines and Real Property in Park County, Colorado, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Gerald Fogarty Galloway Collection of Records Concerning Mines and Real Property in Park County, Colorado. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Upon initial accessioning of the collection in 1997, library staff placed the majority of oversize material into broadside folders and made no effort to arrange it. In 2014, library staff created lists of the oversize material as housed during accessioning, and minimally arranged other material.

bioghist.head: Gerald Fogarty Galloway (1886-1975)
bioghist.p: Gerald "Jerry" Fogarty Galloway was born in Epping, England, son of a medical doctor Arthur Wilton Galloway (1849-1908) and Louisa Warde Fogarty Galloway (1851-1924). He immigrated to the United States in 1901, graduated from Colorado University in 1914, and subsequently taught at Rice University. In 1918, Galloway moved to Alma, Colorado, where he worked as an independent civil mining engineer, as well as a county surveyor and county supervisor for Park County. He also became active in several local mining ventures.

In his work, Galloway collected and maintained real property records for Park County created before he began his tenure as county surveyor. This includes the earliest records created by Lee Hayes (1844-1946), a mining engineer and United States deputy mineral surveyor established in the region circa 1880. In 1884, Hayes sold his business to engineers Hiram Phillips (1859-1921) and William H. Powless (1853-1949); the latter consequently served as county surveyor. During his tenure, Powless surveyed many of the region's roads and town plats represented in the collection, as well as established water rights in Park County. In 1919, Powless sold his business to Galloway, who likely acquired Hayes's and Powless's files at that time.
In 1917, Galloway married Mary "Molly" Montague (1896-1990), and they had a son, Gerald Fogarty Galloway, Jr. (born circa 1924).

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: Material collected and created by Gerald F. Galloway including maps, field notes, correspondence, and other papers related to mining and the development of real property in Park County, Colorado, circa 1872-circa 1970. The bulk of material in the collection relates to gold, silver, lead, zinc, and uranium mines, as well as the companies responsible for mining operations. The collection also documents the infrastructure of Park County, including roads and water works in the county seat, Fairplay, and the statutory town of Alma, as well as the communities of Bailey, Como, Grant, Guffey, Hartsel, Jefferson, Lake George, and Shawnee. It also details changes in areas that became ghost towns, including Antero Junction, Buckskin Joe (also known as Laurette or Lauret), Garo, Howbert, and Tarryall. The collection documents the surveying work of Galloway, as well as his predecessors in the regions, including Fred C. Morse, Lee Hayes, Hiram Phillips, and William H. Powless.

Personal papers related to Galloway in the collection include his schoolwork from the Colorado University and a correspondence course at Columbia University in the City of New York, as well as correspondence with friends and extended family members.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Indices, circa 1918-circa 1970. II. Volumes, circa 1872-1928. III. Numbered Files, circa 1872-circa 1970. IV. Working Files, circa 1920-circa 1970. V. Personal Papers, circa 1910-circa 1970. VI. Broadside Material, 1889-1969. VII. Rolled Material, 1882-1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of indices that document other material in the collection. The entries in the indices include more descriptive information, such as geographic locations, than provided in this guide to the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume includes: list of numbered papers (1-983); index to lodes (A-Z), which includes related maps, books, and papers, it lacks pages W-Z; index of books (1-61); and an incomplete list of surveys (37-5548)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume includes: list of numbered books (1-186); list of large maps (1-173), list of papers (1-2478); list of lodes (A-Z); index of mill sites; index of named placers (A-Z); United States locating monuments; ditches and reservoirs; ranch, coal, and etc. lands; town sites; roads and railroads; school and commissioner's districts; transportation; and notes about townships, voting precincts, and county lines

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: Survey Volumes, Letterpress Copybooks, and Other Volumes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II consists of volumes that chiefly document survey related to real property in Park County, Colorado.
More decriptive information, such as geographic locations, about many of the volumes is available in the volumes that form Series I. Indices.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by the number of the volume followed by unnumbered volumes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of books that include field notes, map sketches, and other information about mines and other locations in Park County, as well as scrapbooks with published patent notices.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: The numbers assigned to survey volumes correlate to numbers recorded in volumes in Series I. Indices.

did.unittitle: No. 27, scrapbook for published patent notices
No. 39, record book of patent surveys
No. 40, by Hiram Phillips and William H. Powless
No. 42, by Hiram Phillips and William H. Powless
No. 84, scrapbook for published patent notices
No. 85, scrapbook for published patent notices
No. 103, scrapbook for published patent notices
No. 114, copy book for stray patents
No. 161, copy book , survey general office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of partially used letterpress copybooks with descriptions of lodes in Park County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of lode descriptions on the first 25 pages of the volume

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of lode descriptions on the first 25 pages of the volume
Label affixed to the front cover: 02

did.unittitle: Plat maps, probably in Park County, Colorado

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large volume includes maps and other material laid in

did.unittitle: Gurden, Richard Lloyd. Traverse Tables: Computed to 4 Places Decimals for Every ́of Angle Up to 100 of Distance. For the Use of Surveyors and Engineers (London: C. Griffin, 1894)

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: The numbers assigned to files correlate to numbers recorded in volumes in Series I. Indices.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged sequentially by file number followed by unnumbered files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III consists of files maintained by Galloway and his predecessors that chiefly relate to mines and lodes in Park County, Colorado. The files includes affidavits, field notes, surveys, maps, plats, correspondence, and other material.
More decriptive information, such as geographic locations, about many of the files is available in the volumes that form Series I. Indices.
Numbered files are not extant for each number within respective sequences. Missing file sequences include: 151-275 (except 158-159), 426-600, 701-725 (except 722), and 1126-1175.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last numbered file 2478

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV consists of files maintained by Galloway that chiefly relate to his work as a civil mining engineer, as well as a surveyor and supervisor for Park County. The files include headings for clients and government agencies, as well as projects, subjects, and places, including lode names and mine names.
These files include preparatory sketches of maps and notes by Galloway on the versos of Park County ballots and other governmental publications.

Palton et al. Geology and Ore Deposits...

United States Department of the Interior Topographic maps of Colorado
Wiser, Ray. The Farm Problem and its Relation to Mining

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers related to Galloway, including correspondence with family and friends, financial records, school records, and manuscript drafts of his creative writing.

did.unittitle: Ancient Free and Accepted Masons, Doric Lodge No. 25, Fairplay, Colorado
Ancient and Accepted Scottish Rite of Free Masonry
Colorado State Board of Registration for Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors
Colorado State Library Services for the Blind and Physically Handicapped
Galloway, Gerald F. "Attempt at a story"
Mountain States Telephone and Telegraph Company
National Library Services for the Blind and Physically Handicapped
Park County Hospital and Nursing Home
Tax receipts for property in Park County, Colorado
University of Wyoming, Western History Research Center

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is arranged as received by the library with unsorted material followed by a group of Park County Water Works Maps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI consists of oversize material that chiefly relate to mines and lodes in Park County, Colorado, including affidavits, field notes, surveys, maps, plats, correspondence, and other material.
Upon accessioning in 1997, library staff placed the material in broadside folders. In most cases, an individual broadside folder contains many items.
In 2014, library staff created a list of the material within in each broadside folder. Not all material has definitive identification.

did.unittitle: Maps of Paris Lode; London Mine workings for first, second, third, fourth, and fifth levels
Deer Valley Road map; Profile of a water pipe line
Sketch of American Mine and part of Russian Mine, as well as unidentified maps that document a plan of section of drill holes and an assay map
Placer map, four sketches, and London Extension detail
Assay of West London Mines and notes; two sketches; maps of Bay Lodes, Surface Workings, North End of London Mines
Maps and field notes on American Extension Trust Mining Claims, and sketch showing production of Extension Winze Stoper
Maps showing position of American Shaft, and detail showing apex of vein and fault

Maps, including Park County school districts, circa 1935; Township No. 13; Shawnee; plat of the property of South Platte Dredging Company; town site of Jefferson; supplement map; Township No. 14; Plat of Dear Valley and Everett and Mildred J. Howard Claimant; and Zulu Chief Mine
Maps of transverse section Block E of American Lode Mine; property claim of Colorado Louisiana Mining Company; Bailey; mining claims on Mount Bross, and sketches
Survey notes and sketches on map

Maps, including plans for Water Supply Development in Colorado Springs; map No. 3 of Water Pipe Line for Colorado Springs and applicant's certificate; Leaves of May Placer claim, and map of location of pipeline
Sketches of former Snyder Ranch for P.F. Berry with correspondence, and topographic maps of the property and meadow
Maps of Colorado Highway, and Bailey and environs
Map of Park County; blueprint of Champaign Mine and sketches of various mining sites
Maps and sketches for Lake George Park
Sketches for mining lodes; resubdivision dedication; map of Wandcrest divisions Numbers 1, 2, and 3; and sketches and field notes for ranch estates

Plan and profile of Colorado State Highway No. 8
Sketches map of Section E-E of American Mine a court case
Property map of J. C. Abell and Kathleen A. Abell at Lake George; survey of 130 Stope London Proper, and a sketch
Notes, sketches, and correspondence about the Platt River Placer
Sketch and blueprint of the longitudinal section for workings of London Mine
Plans for Colorado State Highway No. 4
Plat of mine workings of Sir Charles Lodes; small survey of 642; Zelma Placer claim of Winrow Gold Mining and Milling Company; notes for lower Golden Era audit; and sketches
Elevation map for Fairplay 110, and sketch for Hartsel Road
Map of section across blocks C and D of London Mine, and sketch tracing of the Huron Lode
Head Gate Ditch priority map; and photocopy map of longitudinal section of the London Vein
Mascotle-Orphan Boy-Philips Mines; map of Water Rights Express Reservoir and Pipe Line in Hill Top Mines; and maps showing workings of Blocks A, B, C, D, and E of London Mine
Maps of McDonald No. 2 vein, and Colorado State Highway Project 791
Plan of workings of Paris Excelsior Claims; details of a machine for mineral recovery; as well as maps of voting precincts in Park County, Magnolia Mine, Lode Mine Claims, and Horseshoe Park
Sketch for Lee Wallace of the former Terhune Ranch in Tarryall, and photocopied survey map of portion of Martin Property
Plat of Insmont; map of Friendship Ranch Unit 5; and sketches
Maps of McKinley subunit 1, Hill and Dale subdivision, and platted roads
Sketches for Cole patents, and Cole Incorporated elevation notes

Topographic map for area adjacent to London Mine Tunnel; longitudinal section of mine workings blocks A, B, C, D, and E; sketches of American Mine; and longitudinal map of the London Mines and Milling Company
Sketches of Silver Wave and Blue River No. 1 placers and map of Spinney Ranch
Plat of mining property operated by Galloway as Red Lion Mines and sketches
Highway and transportation maps of Park County
Map of American Mine at 'J' Fault from field book of 1933; and plat of Mineral Park Placer
Exhibit of London Mine for a court case; map of lands owned by C. J. Abell and Kathleen A. Abell; and sketch, markings for corners of a subdivision
Sketch of dump on Russia Lode; sketch of raises at London Mine; and map for Horace Greeley Shaw
Public notice on school bond election for Park County; and sketch, floor plan for South Park High School
Maps of Iron Blossom lodes; claim map of Golden Acres; and map of the Golden Acres lodes group
Architectural drawings for a building; sketch and plat of lots and tracts on South Side Front Street in Fairplay; and sketch of Jefferson Lake/Pacific Mine
Notes on Fairplay Dam Disposal Project; sketches and map related to court case, Park Range Mining and Prospecting Company vs. London Mines and Milling Company ; and plan of Fairplay Dam and Spillway
Hartsel Ranch map; Orphan Boy Placer affidavit; and Park County School District map
Profile of pipeline for Fairplay Water Works; photocopy of plat of Monte Cristo lode and mill site from 1880; and sketches

Blueprint drawing of mine Workings of White Swan No. 1, White Swan No. 2, and Birthday Lodes; and lode claims of Lone Jack Incorporated
Sketch for American Extension Trust; map of Park County; map of voting precincts for Park County; Park County Highway map; and map of the Puma Reserve
Block E Workings for American Mine; and survey maps
Sketches and blueprint drawings, including West London Mines property plat with adjacent claims and details
Blueprint of Jefferson Lake Reservoir, as well as copies and related sketches
Additional amended plat for Freshwater with notes; and maps for the elevation and underground workings of Normal to Hock Hocking Vein

Sketches and maps, including map of Alma; plat of Bryan Placer Patents; blueprint drawing of stopes for American Mine; geography of Leadville atlas sheets; map of Horseshoe District for Leadville Corporation; map of Bailey and environs; and survey of Conflict, Saturn, and Peerless Fraction lodes

Plat of Niagara and Bushwhacker lodes; plat of Mine Workings upon Black Flag Group; boundary plat of Oliviera and S.J. Phillips Placer Mines; map of Alma Placer Permits; map of Park County; and drawing of details for machine
American South End Line sketches; and drilling map of London Tunnel
Blueprint drawing of North London Mill Distribution Line; blueprint plan of American Lode Mine; and map of McCartney Seepage Ditch, as well as sketches
Sketches and surveys of Champaign Dump No. 3
Sketch for Road Tarryall Reservoir, and maps of Priority No. 2, Donnell No. 2 Reservoir, and Donnell Reservoir
Cross section of London Mine, and map for Mosquito Range Mining Region
Sketches and Colorado state highway maps
Blueprint drawings of Shelby Mine and Magnolia Mine; and survey map of mines owned by Fluorspar, Inc.
Plats of the Consolidated Goldacres claim; and maps of the Goldacres Group and Iron Blossom and related lodes
Maps and sketches of Charmetella Park and Royal Park
Map of water in Park County; and highway and transportation map of Gilpin County and Creek County
Maps of Little Mint Placer Mine; '26' for Slater Investments; Milligan Lakes Quadrangle; Little Belle and related lodes; Fairplay; Storming Jordan Placer Mine; and mine workings of the London Mine
Newspaper clippings chiefly related to mines and projects in Park County

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by the map number probably applied by Galloway.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Set of maps related to water works in Park County, 1909-1955. Some maps have multiple copies.
The maps were formerly in a roll. In 2014, library staff placed the maps in a single broadside folder that fills an entire broadside drawer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VII is organized into two subseries: Small Rolls, and Large Rolls.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VII consists of material accessioned and chiefly retained as rolls that relate to mines and lodes in Park County, Colorado, including affidavits, field notes, surveys, maps, plats, correspondence, and other material.
Upon accessioning in 1997, library staff retained material in rolls. In 2014, library staff created a list of the material within each subseries.
In many cases, a single roll contains several items.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as accessioned by library staff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short rolls of material housed in oversize boxes.

did.unittitle: Maps of downtown Friscoe and Junction 1; as well as field notes and correspondence with Jesse Horn
Map of basic geography and mining lodes

Plat of workings on Union No. 5, Pierce and Simeon 166; a geology map of the Vanadium region of southwest Colorado and southeast Utah; correspondence with W.H. Powless about Red Lion, Union S, Golden Era, and Sir Charles

Map of Queen Extension; and sketches and map of placer locations
Map of lodes and placers near Buckskin
Assay map of samples from London and London Extension in the American Mine
Map of Diamond Drill Holes (Plat No. 2); and map of Plat No. 3 London Gold Mines Company (Plat No. 3)
Map of Mosquito Creek and Pennsylvania Mountain
Maps of American Mine, including 200 level, 400 level, and South End; topographical map of London Mine Tunnel and Frisbee Placer; elevation and map of the London Mines and Milling Company
Map of Bell and Allegheny Placer locations; map of Breyton in Greeley County, Nebraska); map of claims between Park and Summit Counties; and sketch of a railing and floor system
Map of Park County; sketches of lots within Colorado Midland Railway
Maps of Ophir Placer and Maple Placers; Mudsill Mine; Wilken-Borden Ranch; and Kentucky Belle
Maps of South London, Vienna, Blanchard tunnels; lode map of Dinero, American, Ibex, Fraction, Huron, Flora A, and Genevieve
Maps of placers near Summit County; survey map of Lane Lode; map of Weehawken Placer, Newport Placer, and Eureka Placer; map of Crescent, Bertha, and Dolphin, Edna, and Clipper; and map of Franny
Map of Current Creek Pass Road
Map of Nelson Lower Ranch and Case Ranch over Kenosha
Correspondence on Chase Gulch Dam project with field notes and survey
Topographical map of Cripple Creek with survey notes
Plat in Blanchard Drift and survey notes
Map of amended plot of Township No. 12 with survey notes
Plan of proposed pipeline and ditch for London Mill Incorporated; map of position of American Shaft; and sketch of fault near American lode and Huron lode
Map of Jefferson to Lake George
Maps of Shelby Mine and Shelby Mine Workings; diagram of line control between Park County and Jefferson County
Plan showing portions of Highway No. 9 across Carey W. Rhodes placers; map of State Road No. 9 through Alma; map of Grosse Placer and Treeweck Placer
Map of Slate Road from Hartsel to McQuaid
Map of the elevation profile from Hilltop to Gulleys
Blueprint drawing of Ptarmigan Park; sketches and map of Hilltop Mine, including workings and a main audit; map of Present Road; map of Last Chance Lode
Survey of Wagon Road; map of Silver Wave Mine at Buckskin Gulch; plat of the ranch of A. DeBernardi; map of spillway for Lake Shawnee
Maps related to Bailey, including maps of Bailey and environs; Entriken Homestead, and assay sketches
Map of workings on upper or middle tunnel of the North London Mine Division
Elevation profile of Jefferson and Lake George
Ling Mine related material, including a diagram of north and south audits, diagrams, and sketches
Map of the Horseshoe Mining District
Elevation profile of the Platte River Valley
Map of Bailey related to Highway 285 and Helvig Svedsen
Map of Alma to Hoosier Pass
Survey map of Weehawken Lode and mining claims related to the Hall Valley Company
Elevation profile map of Bunnells Crossing to Hartsel Buena Vista Road
Map of road from Jefferson to Lake George
Map and survey notes on Jefferson
Sketches, including Havighorst Tunnel and London Mine
Geological cross sections of the Red Mountain District, Sneffels District, and Telluride District
Maps of the South London tunnel at various levels

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as accessioned by library staff.

did.unittitle: Sketches of Alma, Florado Mine in Montezuma, Colorado
Longitudinal section of London Mine Workings Blocks A, B, C, D, and E, and sketches for a proposed Pipe Line for Mill London Mine Inc.
Sketches for Caylor Placer and Lake George
Sketches of Iola, Paris, Emma Nevada, and Mater claims
Plans and sketches for the Fluorspar Inc. Mill
Maps, sketches, and notes for Grant, Colorado
Maps, sketches, and notes of Fairplay, Colorado, including playgrounds, and water works
Maps for Last Chance Mining Company; tramway for Dolly Varden Mine; and the Bronlow and Stephens ditches, as well as a sketch of the Dolly, Russia, and Moose Mines
Sketches and exhibits for the London Mine
Map and sketches of the Criterion mine
Map and sketch for of the old Nelson Ranch for Walt Berry and others
Map of Paris, Iola, and Wisconsin lodes
Cross sections of the American Mine

Material chiefly related to the London Mines Company, including sketches of a boarding house; topographical map of properties; plan for a proposed pond for mill tailings; plat of mill buildings; and material related to a conveyor, as well as a map of the American Mine Tram Line
Plat of Shawnee Park; survey notes and sketches of Insmont; and protective covenants for Friendship Ranch
Map showing stopes in London Extension; transverse section north of 'J' Fault Line; diamond drill record and core vertical section for the West London Mine; and sketches of the London Mine
Map and studies of Mineral Park
Diagram of Park-Jefferson county line control
Sketches, including American Mines, proposed mill site for London Mine; and proposed pipeline for mill tailings for London Mine Inc.
Sketches for Alma GT Placer portion, and map of the Colorado State Road No. 9 between Alma and Breckinridge
Maps, including block D of the London Mines Co.
Map and sketch of Magnolia Mine
Sketch of London Mine and Shelby Mine
Survey conducted near Park and Jefferson county line, and photographs of mine workings
Topographical map of Circle V Bar Ranch
